SUBJECT: F5 Multi-Cloud/Hybrid Environments
CONTENT:
Hi Jamie,

I really liked this analogy. The most resilient teams are not the ones who never fail. They are the ones who plan for it.

What I see most often is that failure responses are still manual. When something breaks, teams scramble across tools and environments to reroute traffic, restore access, and reapply security while users feel the impact.

This is why some teams use F5 Distributed Cloud as a front door for internet facing apps and APIs. It removes dependence on a single site or region so when something degrades, traffic and security behavior are already accounted for. Policies continue to enforce and users stay connected even while the underlying issue is fixed.

The mindset shift is not just how do we stay up, but what continues to work when something underneath fails. The best designs assume the stumble and build the recovery in advance.

Do you have time this month to discuss how the secure, transparent, seamless connection of F5's Distributed Cloud can benefit the tech stack at HDR?

Cheers,


Gustave Lorge

Dan

Just checking in to see how life is going - hoping everything is good!
Here is a listing you might be interested in also. https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/4381693769/

I totally get that! I’m nervous about what kind of director and manager we will get in here, because they will really drive the direction. We really need someone who can listen to the employees, involve them in the decision, and get buy in once decisions are made. That doesn’t seem like such a hard ask, but it’s been such a top-down “do it because I said so” approach, and that’s what’s made everyone so dissatisfied here. Not all of their decisions have been bad, but people are still mad about them because of the approach. That was mostly from the former director, so hopefully that will change regardless. 

I definitely don’t want you to apply if you are happy where you are. I just know that you’re the type of person that could make a positive impact here, and trust me when I say, good managers are hard to find! I also know that I only have to keep my head down and ride out the storm for 3-5 years and I can do that with anyone. I think they will eventually eliminate a lot of the telecom staff once things get moving, but I’m trying to be helpful enough to remain relevant at that time. I’m currently the only telecom person on the 18 person (some are vendors) meeting for upgrading the phone system (that should send alarm bells). 

All that to say- don’t worry about me! Do what feels right for you! It may pay more, but it may be a lot more stress too. If you’re comfortable where you are and see a long-term future as a possibility there, then there’s nothing wring with that!
I love Sean! That’s awesome! I am so torn about this. I’d love to work with you again but right now its “the devil you know”. 😁

We have  quite a few project managers. Sean Powers is one of them! They don’t get involved in every single project, but if it’s high profile or touches multiple departments they do. We had several involved in the Cisco phone project. One main one, one helping him, and another from the vendor.

Funny, we are working on a plan right now to get out of Tierpoint. 
Do you guys have project managers that help wrangle these projects or is it on the teams themselves to organize them?

Upper management here has been a mess, but the majority of them are gone now. One person any former network employee might complain about is still here though. She is now the CIO and Exec Director of Infrastructure (last part is new). He name is Lisa Bazis and she has a way of rubbing people the wrong way. She reminds me a little of Kelly Canova, so if you didn’t mind Kelly, you’d be fine with her. The Director position would report to her in the new org chart. The previous Exec Director was Jeff Bergholz. He worked here a long time, and I’m not actually sure why he was fired other than he was opposed to the cloud and wasn’t overly forward thinking. The Director position would be over Data Center Operations, Telecom, Network, and the Server team (also possibly Info Sec as that was Lisa’s group and she was promoted to the new title). There’s a possibility that they will promote an Info Sec manager to this Director position, because Lisa has him sort of doing that job (unofficially) now. But there are clear signs he may not be the best choice for that (clear to workers, but maybe not management), so it’s hard telling! I think a couple of network guys are applying for manager. No idea how that will go. None of the Telecom people are applying for that. The positions have been open for a week I think. No idea how long they will keep them open. Sometimes they are open forever and sometimes they close them to go through applicants. 

The Director position would need to deal with the telephone system upgrade, the move to HyperV for the server team, a possible move of the data center from Tierpoint to another (not chosen yet) location, and helping to unravel some billing messes we have. It’s a lot, but I do honestly think you would be a good fit (in either position really). I totally understand anyone who doesn’t want to deal with all of that too! The time constraints are the stressful part, but once a plan is in place hopefully things will fall in order. Dare to dream!

That sounds like it’s a bit of a mess over there. I have a couple people that worked there on the Network team on my team now and the stories they have told me about the upper management aren’t great. What is the name of the VP that’s is over that area? 
I will say that $ you listed is more than I’m making now but to be honest, I’d be really interested in the Director position if anything. And the WFH portion sounds amazing. 
I’m going to have to think about this a lot more. Thank you for all this info!

I’m not sure yet how the UNMC/Clarkson/NM thing will impact us. I think it will take 1 1/2 to 2 years to see any big changes. I was UNMC for 20 years before they sold us to NM (and we had the pleasure of reapplying for our jobs). The current changes in IT itself are more imminent and we just don’t know how things will play out. 

Last summer they fired the CTO who hadn’t been here long. He stopped doing any actual work (wasn’t forwarding contracts to sign, missing meetings, etc). In November they fired our VP and Director (the Director isn’t missed), and in January the network, server team, and telecom managers all resigned. Not necessarily because they don’t agree with the others being gone (my boss went with early retirement, because his wife has stage 4 cancer that’s metastasized from a prior cancer), but the new interim in charge is trying to shake things up. The server manager quit, because they are doing a knee-jerk reaction to price increases from VMware and saying we need to convert to HyperV in just a few months. 

Telecom is a bit of a mess - we were going all Cisco and I led the project to convert Bellevue Medical Center from a key system to Cisco (worst project I’ve ever worked on). When they fired the director (who was pushing it) I appealed to the VP that temp inherited us to put a hold on the project for main campus, because the manner they were going about it was awful. She agreed and we put it on hold for a bit, but now she’s declared that we are not renewing the Avaya contract that’s up in a year and a half, so we’re talking to our business partners about options (my guess is we’ll do a hybrid of Cisco and Teams). They decided to move the telecom engineers (my group) to the network team as a result (not combine us back with the telecom analyst and tech team). We are all leaderless though, so decisions aren’t being made, and time is ticking on the Avaya contract. 

The network team has been taking heat for the switch upgrades they’ve been doing (the hospital staff seems they haven’t been informed enough about things that go down), so they are stressed (they had the same bad director we did, so it’s not a surprise). We don’t know how we fit in with their team yet, and since neither one of us have a manger right now the groups are just doing our own things under the direction of the leads. 

Some people think it’s all doom and gloom, but I’m oddly excited about some of the opportunities if they are done correctly (which means getting good managers and a director in place). I might be a masochist. Ha!! Or it might me wishful thinking since the job market isn’t great elsewhere. Time will tell! 

I think the manager position pays around $180-200k (there are 6 telecom engineers, and about a dozen network engineers they will manage), but that’s a semi-educated guess. The director would obviously be more. I don’t think they will have most of IT be back in the office full-time any time soon. There’s no room for us. Some managers go in 1-2 days a week, because they do have on-site employees (like the technicians and pc support). I definitely do like working from home. I honestly get a lot more done, and I don’t have to deal with traffic, weather, and office drama! I don’t even have a desk at the med center when I do have to go in, but that’s fine with me. I also hope I only have another 3-5 years to work, so I’d like to finish out my glory days at home! Ha!

I’m flattered! Thank you for thinking of me! I’ll take a look at these positions but right now I’m just over a year in at HDR and am pretty happy with how things are going. (And how they pay 😝). I do have to be in the office 5 days a week but so far it’s not horrible. 
I miss working with you and if we weren’t 100% in office you’d be at the top of my list for people I’d actively recruit. 
How are things over there? I know there’s been a pretty big shake up and then everything that’s going on with the whole UNMC/clarkson/Nebraska medicine thing. How are you doing?

Hey there! I’m not sure if anyone ever contacted you about Teams. We are still considering that, or a hybrid solution. In the meantime, my manager, Tim, has retired and they moved the Telecom Engineers to the Network team. My lead, James (also interviewed when I did), said “no one has the experience they are looking for,” and in my head I thought - I know someone who does! So…. If you’re bored at your job and looking for a challenge integrating a telecom and network group and “ushering in a new era of modernization” (their words, not mine!) at a company that’s going through a takeover, then I’ve got just the job for you! https://nebraskamed.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/NM/job/XMLNAME-10787---Manager-Enterprise-Digital-Infrastructure-and-Connectivity--Exempt-_REQ-34660

The second one wouldn’t give you the opportunity to work with both me and James directly, but it does have more influence (basically like Scott V’s position). 

So if you’re looking for a new opportunity - both are mostly remote (1 day a month management retreats on-site and a few other days here and there), have unlimited PTO, pay well, and have decent benefits (not the least of which is working with me! Ha). I NEED A GOOD MANAGER AGAIN. 

Anyway- hope you’re doing well!

CONVERSATION TITLE: Come work for Google! Data Center Area Lead- Council Bluffs or Lincoln
SUBJECT: Come work for Google! Data Center Area Lead- Council Bluffs or Lincoln
CONTENT:
Hi Jamie!

My name is Aaron from Google’s Technical Infrastructure recruiting team. Your leadership experience in large-scale technical operations stood out to me for a high-impact opportunity we currently have supporting our data centers in Council Bluffs, Iowa or Lincoln, Nebraska.

We are looking for an Area Lead to act as the General Manager for this region. In this role, you wouldn’t just manage a team; you would assume full accountability for the operational success of a massive data center portfolio—balancing OPEX management and performance metrics with a relentless commitment to site safety and culture.

This position offers the autonomy to lead a distributed team of site leaders who maintain the hardware and networking protocols powering Google’s core services. We’re ideally seeking a leader with a strong technical foundation in server lifecycles and Linux/Unix systems who thrives on driving strategic consistency across a complex, matrixed environment.

If this sounds of interest, please share your latest resume and I'll share further details on the opportunity and we can go from there!

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Best,
Aaron

Aaron Nahil
Lead Technical Recruiter @ Google

SUBJECT: Immediate Opportunity: Network Operations Manager
CONTENT:

CONVERSATION TITLE: Network Engineer - Full-Time Permanent Position - Available - Omaha, NE
SUBJECT: Network Engineer - Full-Time Permanent Position - Available - Omaha, NE
CONTENT:
Hello:

We reviewed your profile on LinkedIn, and your profile is OUTSTANDING! We have a full-time permanent Network Engineer position available. Location: Omaha, NE

* Position is Onsite everyday Monday - Friday.
* C2C is not an option for the position.
* Remote is not an option for the position.

Required Qualifications: 
•     Design, implement, and maintain enterprise network infrastructure including routing and switching across Cisco and Aruba platforms.
•     Develop and maintain automation scripts and templates using Ansible to improve network efficiency and reduce manual tasks.
•     Manage Aruba SD-WAN, ClearPass, and wireless systems (Aruba & Cisco) to ensure seamless connectivity and secure access control.
•     Administer Cisco ACI fabric in the data center environment, including integration with Palo Alto firewalls.
•     Monitor network health and performance using tools such as SolarWinds, Cisco DNA Center, and Nexus Dashboard Insights.
•     Utilize Infoblox for DNS, DHCP, and IPAM services.

Preferred Skills:
     •     Cisco and Aruba networking technologies, Aruba SD-WAN, wireless infrastructure, Cisco ACI, and Palo Alto firewalls, network monitoring, automation, and IP management tools
     •     Cisco and Aruba routing/switching, including VLANs, STP, BGP, OSPF, EIGRP, and VRFs.
     •     Aruba SD-WAN, Aruba ClearPass, and enterprise wireless technologies.
     •     Cisco ACI, including contracts, EPGs, tenants, and service graphing.
     •     Palo Alto Next-Gen Firewalls, including policy creation and troubleshooting.
     •     SolarWinds, Cisco DNA Center, and Nexus Dashboard for performance monitoring and analytics.
     •     Infoblox for IP address management and DNS/DHCP configuration.
     •     Solid automation skills using Ansible for configuration management and task automation.

Are you free to chat this week?

Sincerely,

Michael Anthony McArdle
Ranked 2025 Top 1% LinkedIn Recruiter, Globally | Candidate Experience Champion | Hiring Partner

SUBJECT: Jamie, hiring?
CONTENT:
Hey Jamie,

I am with NCube, a service company providing staff augmentation for over 14 years in Ukraine. The developer community in Ukraine is over 300,000 strong. We’re ready to work. If you have a need, support us by letting us support you.

We've been through many crises, and have been able to fight and strive through them all. A full-scale war in Ukraine is the toughest, but we were ready for it. All of our developers have been relocated to safe zones. With the power outages because of rocket shelling, we have put power generators and Starlink systems to ensure uninterrupted work of our teams. 

On top of that, we started growing outside Ukraine. We have opened development centers in the EU (Poland, Romania, Bulgaria, Portugal) and LATAM (Brazil).

If you are planning to expand your team, let's have a quick chat. Or shall I just send you some CVs to take a look at?

Best regards,
Greg

CONTENT:
Hello Jamie, 

LTI is hiring Network Engineer in Portland, OR. If you are interested, please share your resume to ksunil.kumar@lntinfotech.com 

100% On-site role. Both Contract/Full-time
 
•Strong experience in a medium/ large scale enterprise networks & 5+ years of relevant experience
•Network and security knowledge on Cisco products (routing, switching and firewalls).
•Strong network troubleshooting skills from layer 1-7.
•Experience with enterprise grade load balancers (e.g. F5 ) and Cisco Access Points
•Experience working on monitoring tools SolarWinds Orion NCM and NPM, Aruba.
•Experience with ACI / F5 Load balancer is a must
•Should have worked on DC network documentation and related processes.
•Should have experience in handling data center network related incidents within defined SLA’s.

•Residential telephone services are built using IMS and VoIP components
•Installation, configuration and strong troubleshooting experience in Cisco devices Experience in Voice, WAN, Wireless & Site Networks maintenance Datacenter support
•Perform network maintenance and system upgrades including service packs, patches, hot fixes, and security configurations.
•Provide Level- 2/ 3 support and troubleshooting to resolve issues within SLA.

Must have Experience in the following technologies
o	Cisco Routers & Switches
o	Cisco WIFI 
o	F5 Load Balancer
o	Palo Alto Firewall & Cisco ASA Firewall
o	WAN Optimizers
o	Cisco VOIP 
 
•Certifications : CCNA & CCNP is preferred

Experience in automation using ansible, python is plus.

Sunil Kumar
Talent Acquisition at LTI - Larsen & Toubro Infotech

CONTENT:
Jamie-

Hope the week is treating you well and so is your current position. On the chance that you’re open to hearing about a new role I’d love to chat with you. 

I work for a next-gen tech company that builds programs, sells modern technology to some of the largest enterprise companies in the country and I happen to run the practice within our business that helps them find people. I am working exclusively with a client of ours hiring for a direct hire Sr. Leader of Network Infrastructure.  This position is incredibly visible and critical for a massive global organization. This person will have two managers reporting to them (one over network architecture and one over operations) and they use a 3rd party for their NetOps Center that you'd manage the relationship with with about 22 people reporting up to the org all together.

They're about 20% migrated towards the cloud and they need someone who has experience driving digital transformation- with a proactive approach to change and cloud enablement. They're a cisco shop and they have a complex enterprise environment. 

The role is an amazing opportunity because this person will really be able to drive organizational change and the hiring manager wants this person to be empowered to make things happen and have the autonomy to get things done in their way. Leadership will have your ear and depend on you for strategy and scalability.

Thriving company in growth mode right now-They want someone in Ohio, San Antonio or Denver due to the high impact nature of the position but they will pay a full old school white glove relocation package and you'd want to be there for the role. The opportunity pays 185-230k+ 25% bonus or more (I have flex since they want the right person) and 25% equity/RSUs, not to mention an old school pension plan on top of 401k match, very generous PTO and growth potential.

Let's chat!

Shera Southerland
Director of Talent Acquisition  at Set Solutions, Inc.
ssoutherland@setsolutions.com

SUBJECT: Are you ready for your next play?  I have an immediate REMOTE contract employment opportunity for a Network Engineer with CCIE OR CCNP Data Center Certification
CONTENT:
I ran across your profile on LinkedIn.  It looks like you have done some great things in the Network Engineering arena. Cisco has an immediate REMOTE contract / contract to perm employment opportunity for a Network Engineer with CCIE or CCNP Data Center Certification (preferably including Lab Cert).   The ideal candidate will have interest in Project Management in a Certification/Exam environment.

Cisco will potentially convert this contract assignment to perm employment and is looking for candidate that do not require sponsorship (US Citizen or Green Card).

This team is involved in the technology every day but they will not be involved in hands on Network Engineering responsibilities.  This would be the next step for someone that wants to move on from a hands-on role but stay in the technology.

-	Building Topology
-	Ordering Equipment for consultants and labs

SUBJECT: Network Engineering Manager Opening @ Acrisure
CONTENT:
Hey there!

Acrisure is a global Financial Technology organization and as we continue to grow we are looking for more and more skilled IT professionals to help us achieve our goals.

I am doing some internal sourcing on a Network Engineering Manager opening that would help multiple areas of our organization support some of our agency partners along with corporate Acrisure in our home office in Downtown Grand Rapids.  At a VERY high level, this is going to be the go to person over top of this group.  You would be the technical leader for a 8 person team and assist with providing the day to day insight, project tasking / planning, and day to day prioritization of the team.  You'd still be jumping in and being technical as needed- but the final escalation point after our tier 3 engineers.  This is a great opportunity to join a fast growing organization and set yourself up for a fast growing position within a global organization.  

This role can be 100% remote outside of quarterly travel to our Grand Rapids (Michigan) HQ!

With competitive salaries and excellent benefits, Acrisure is quickly becoming one of the destination employers in the Greater GR area. I would love to connect and learn a little more about your background and career interests to see if this would be something you'd be a good fit for (and interested in!).

Lets connect!

Chris Holka
IT Talent Acquisition @ Acrisure

SUBJECT: L3 DDoS Protection
CONTENT:
Hi Jamie, 

Curious how your current infrastructure security is prepared for DDoS/ransomware attack attempts. Would love to share with you what Cloudflare has designed for network layer protection against DDoS.

Magic Transit tl;dr:
- leverages Cloudflare’s entire Anycast network (121+ Tbps) to mitigate attacks closer to the attack source

- improves performance vs. the added latency tax you often see with traditional scrubbing providers. This means teams can deploy in a proactive "Always On” mode and see boosted network speed

- includes an L4 firewall to enforce security logic at our edge instead of inside your data center

Would love understand more about your infrastructure projects/goals for the remainder of the year to see if we can help. Are you open to an intro? 

Thank you, 
Steph


Stephanie Teissier

It is going well.  Actually I am having fun being semi-retired : ) I have not even started looking for anything.  The strange thing is that people are contacting me out of the blue.  I was going to wait until after my son graduates, which is this next weekend, to really start looking.  BTW DTN's firing process isnt any better than the rest of their HR processes.  They still havent paid me my last paycheck after almost 2 months.  Say HI to the crew for me.  I hope everthing is going well.  Stay intouch, its good to talk to you
Was thinking about you this weekend and thought I'd reach out and see how you're holding up. Any leads anywhere?
Thank you.  I may take you up on that
I can imagine! Keep in touch. My offer stands to be available to talk.
I have had a bad feeling about it for awhile, but the timing was a shock
Were there any signs that it was coming or was it as much a surprise as it was the rest of us?
I am doing ok, I just talked to Dan Fangman and he doing ok.  I may reach out.  Now I got to figure out what is next :)
Hey, how are you doing? That was unexpected! Feel free to reach out to me if you still have my cell phone number. 402.968.1327 or at my home email jamie.sobczyk@gmail.com
I just got my first dose at the Hy Vee in Papillion. I was able to register online and appointments were open. My asthma was also a plus. Try with them and they might take you too.

My family is doing well. My mom just got her second vaccine shot on the 17th. My wife has both of her shots since she's a PA and my daughter has both of hers since she works in a hospital. I'm registered and waiting for my turn. :) 
That is a great commitment that you've made to take care of your mom. I'm sure she appreciates all of the time you are spending with her.
How is your family BTW?

She's doing relatively well. She was diagnosed with dementia, but the influence is light at this time and her treatments are keeping it in relative check. Still, I made a conscious decision to take care of her full time and keep her out of a nursing home because I knew with Covid that it could have been a death sentence if I had put her in there for longer than the rehab stint. She got her two Covid vaccine doses in February and I get my first dose tomorrow.
Thanks for reaching out to me. It's good to hear from you. It seems both longer and shorter than a year since my dad passed. How is your mom doing?

Hey there Jamie, sorry to hear your dad passed almost a year ago. I remember seeing him at Hillcrest when I was there looking after my mom. Then one day he was gone, presumably to hospice care. Then when Covid lockdowns took place, I completely lost touch as I was trying to keep my mom safe and recovering well at home. I didn't know he had passed until late last year when I saw his cross on the wall at church. I'm kind of torn up that I didn't know about it as I so wanted to help you say goodbye to him amigo.
He's doing okay for now. Treatment is showing some signs of working but there's a long way to go. He's still up and moving, going to work every day so that's good.
Surviving. First of all, how is your dad?
Absolutely! How have you been? It seems like forever since we spoke or saw each other.
Nice to see you old friend. If you know of someone who needs a tech writer or just plain writer, let me know. JMC
